commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is live Slack proof or an explicit maintainer decision to accept the local proof.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff only changes in-process Slack/auto-reply routing state and tests, with no dependency, workflow, secret, or code-execution surface changes. (sha=323e3caa96b845ab9ac07a801c2ed2be06dd35f9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR stores routed thread ids on reply operations and suppresses active-run steering when a same-session Slack DM turn belongs to a different Slack message thread.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main keeps Slack DM turns on a stable SessionKey while active steering ignores MessageThreadId, and the PR body reports the before-fix assertion failure.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is live Slack proof or an explicit maintainer decision to accept the local proof.

target.title: fix(slack): keep DM thread turns out of active steering
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve OpenClaw's existing stable Slack DM `SessionKey` contract for top-level DMs and ordinary DM thread replies.
- Track the routed thread id on every active reply operation path, including agent-runner-owned, dispatch-owned, and queued follow-up operations.
- Suppress steering when a new Slack DM turn has the same session key but a different Slack thread id, so overlapping DM threads do not append into each other's active run.

## Verification

- Confirmed latest `origin/main` at `1d1a7c26d8867fdfa98c97d8b510ad0ce1951858` still does not pass `routeThreadId` when `dispatch-from-config.ts` or `followup-runner.ts` create reply operations.
- Watched the original regression fail before the fix: `expected true to be false` for `call.shouldSteer`, proving the second Slack DM thread would still steer into the active run.
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t "records routed Slack thread id|records queued thread id"`
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Two overlapping top-level Slack DMs from the same user should keep the stable DM `SessionKey`, but the second DM thread must not steer into the active run for the first DM thread. Dispatch-owned and queued follow-up reply operations must also record the routed Slack thread id so active-run steering can compare the active operation thread against the current Slack thread.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `323e3caa96b845ab9ac07a801c2ed2be06dd35f9` rebased onto `origin/main` `1d1a7c26d8867fdfa98c97d8b510ad0ce1951858` on macOS, using the repo's `pnpm` test runner against the real OpenClaw auto-reply runtime path and Slack prepare path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: I exercised the same local runtime path used after Slack prepare: an active reply operation for stable DM session `session-key` with routed Slack thread `500.000`, followed by a second Slack direct-message turn on the same `SessionKey` with `MessageThreadId=501.000`; I also verified dispatch-created and follow-up-created reply operations record `routeThreadId=501.000`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw runtime run after this patch:

```text
shouldSteer = false
shouldFollowup = true
isActive = true
isStreaming = false
originatingThreadId = 501.000

$ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t "records routed Slack thread id|records queued thread id"
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 203 skipped (205)

$ pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  275 passed (275)
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  95 passed (95)

$ pnpm check:changed
exit code 0

$ git diff --check
exit code 0
```

- Observed result after fix: Stable Slack DM routing remains intact (`agent:main:slack:direct:u3` for both top-level DMs), while the active-run decision for the second DM thread reports `shouldSteer=false` and keeps `originatingThreadId=501.000`; dispatch-owned and queued follow-up operations now also expose the routed thread id on `replyOperation.routeThreadId`.
- What was not tested: I did not include a recorded external Slack workspace run in this PR body. The proof above is copied local OpenClaw runtime output plus Slack prepare/dispatch/follow-up-path output from this checkout; live Slack workspace proof can still be added by a maintainer/Mantis run if required.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the same local runtime scenario failed with `AssertionError: expected true to be false` at `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts:1306`.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Slack DMs intentionally share the stable direct-message session key, but the active-run steering decision only compared session identity. A second top-level DM from the same user could therefore steer into the active run for the first Slack message thread. The first PR revision recorded routed thread ids only for agent-runner-owned operations, leaving dispatch-owned and queued follow-up operations without the thread metadata used by the steering guard.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Slack prepare tests covered the stable DM session contract, but auto-reply routing tests did not cover same-session, different-thread active runs or every operation creation path.
- Contributing context: The fix belongs at the active-run routing boundary, not in Slack session-key generation, because Slack DM session continuity is a shipped contract.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this: Unit test plus Slack prepare-path contract test.
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`, and `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Same stable Slack DM session, different routed Slack thread id, active prior run, second turn must not steer into the first run; every created active reply operation should carry the routed thread id when one exists.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the auto-reply decision that caused the overlap while separately preserving the Slack session-key contract and covering the dispatch/follow-up operation creation paths ClawSweeper flagged.
target.author: guanbear

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
